en_title: Waiting For One To Wipe Away Tears
en_body:
Last night there came a cradle of sorrow
Passing my lonely attic-room, softly asking:
Asking whether my heart loves anyone?
No, no...yes, yes...
Restlessly turning, shadows drenched,

This morning, the sunburst blooms
Its aureole splendidly radiant, glorious
sutras, prayers...
Nam Mô—the echo of Di-Đà
My heart believed no human shadow haunted the dream
The cradle of sorrow bewildered as mist
Dissolving slowly, yet clouds drench the layered trees
Early wind trembles through leaves and branches
Dim drops quiver upon several plumes of reed-grass.
note: “Nam Mô” invokes Amitābha Buddha, called A-Di-Đà in Vietnamese Pure Land Buddhism.
